text.title: Kubernetes Resource Requests and Limits Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Resource Requests and Limits`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: requests.cpu MUST be at least p95 observed usage; requests.memory MUST be at least p99 observed usage.

rationale.source: Google SRE workbook ch. 12, internal observability practice

rule.rationale: Requests below p95 starve pods on bursts; requests above p99 over-pay scheduler.

rule.statement: For latency-sensitive workloads limits.memory MUST equal requests.memory (Guaranteed QoS for memory).
rule.rationale: Differing memory request/limit gives Burstable QoS; pod is OOM-killed first on node pressure.

rule.statement: limits.cpu SHOULD NOT be set tighter than 2x request for latency-sensitive workloads; better to omit CPU limit entirely.

rationale.source: Kubernetes CPU throttling docs, multiple SRE blogs

rule.rationale: CPU limits introduce throttling spikes that hurt p99 latency disproportionately.

rule.statement: requests.cpu and requests.memory MUST be greater than 0; BestEffort QoS in production is forbidden.
rule.rationale: BestEffort pods are evicted first on pressure; production needs at least Burstable.

rule.statement: Resource requests/limits MUST be reviewed quarterly against observed metrics; stale values are flagged.
rule.rationale: Traffic shape drifts; quarterly review keeps right-sizing honest.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
